description: Family letter from the end of the 12th century to Abu al-Faraj b. Abu al-Barakāt, a doctor in Fustat, from his brother in Alexandria. The letter mentions forceful collection of the capitation tax (jaliya) in Alexandria. (Information from Frenkel. See also Goitein, MEd. Soc. 2:372, 2:372, and 4:61. Goitein describes the document as following: Family letter from a physician in Alexandria to his brother, also a physician, who had traveled to Fustat without having paid his poll-tax (or having forgotten to send the receipt back). The writer had to pay a certain sum every day to the Muslim authorities (tarsim) until he would pay the poll-tax for his brother. After two days of refusing to do so, he was confined to prison and paid the poll-tax.
tags: capitation tax, tax, jaliya, personal, jizya
description_he: מכתב משפחתי מסוף המאה ה-12 לאבו אל-פרג' בן אבו אל-ברכאת, רופא בפוסטאט, מאחיו באלכסנדריה. מוזכרת גביית מס הגולגולת בכפייה. הכותב נאלץ לשלם סכום יומי לרשויות המוסלמיות ולאחר יומיים הושלך לכלא.
